goal: Perform comprehensive LIMMA-based differential expression analysis on the metabolomics dataset (2059 metabolites ×18 samples) across three experimental groups — NC (healthy control), CD (C. difficile infection alone), and FE (high iron diet pretreatment + CDI). The analysis aims to: (1) identify metabolites significantly altered across all groups via moderated F-test (ANOVA), (2) identify metabolites specifically altered by high iron diet in the context of CDI (FE vs CD, the primary comparison), (3) establish the baseline metabolic signature of CDI (CD vs NC), and (4) evaluate the overall protective metabolic phenotype (FE vs NC). The analysis integrates PLS-DA VIP scores from Module2 as an additional ranking criterion for metabolomics feature selection, and leverages the metabolites.csv annotation file for downstream biological interpretation.
output_files: G:/OmicsWorks/test/metabolism/demo/results/tables/
- anova_overall_f_test_results.csv (Moderated F-test across all3 groups)
- diff_FE_vs_CD_all.csv (Full LIMMA results for FE vs CD)
- diff_FE_vs_CD_significant.csv (Significant: adj.P.Val <0.05, top500 by |logFC|)
- diff_CD_vs_NC_all.csv (Full LIMMA results for CD vs NC)
- diff_CD_vs_NC_significant.csv (Significant: adj.P.Val <0.05, top500 by |logFC|)
- diff_FE_vs_NC_all.csv (Full LIMMA results for FE vs NC)
- diff_FE_vs_NC_significant.csv (Significant: adj.P.Val <0.05, top500 by |logFC|)
- combined_diff_results.csv (Merged table with all comparisons + annotations)
- vip_integrated_results.csv (LIMMA results merged with PLS-DA VIP scores)
- top500_logFC_ranked.csv (Top500 molecules ranked by |logFC| across comparisons)

step_1_data_preparation.description: Load preprocessed expression matrix, sample info, and metabolite annotations. Ensure all sample IDs match between expression matrix and sample info. Exclude QC samples (not in expression matrix). Verify matrix is in log2 scale and median-centered (already confirmed in Module1).

step_2_design_matrix_construction.description: Build a linear model design matrix for the three-group comparison. Since this is a cross-sectional study with no time series, a simple one-way ANOVA design (~0 + group) is used. The design will have3 coefficients: NC, CD, FE.
step_2_design_matrix_construction.notes: No time covariate — this is a single time-point cross-sectional study. No blocking factors needed — all samples processed together.

step_3_multi_factor_anova_overall_f_test.description: Fit the linear model using lmFit() and compute moderated F-statistics using eBayes(). The overall F-test tests the null hypothesis that the mean expression of each metabolite is equal across all three groups (NC = CD = FE). This identifies metabolites with ANY significant between-group variation, serving as a global screening step.
step_3_multi_factor_anova_overall_f_test.expected_output: anova_overall_f_test_results.csv with columns: molecule, F_statistic, P.Value, adj.P.Val (FDR)
step_3_multi_factor_anova_overall_f_test.expected_findings: Based on Module2 ANOVA results, approximately1,149 metabolites (55.8%) are expected to be significant at FDR <0.05. These will serve as the universe for downstream pairwise comparisons and pathway enrichment.

step_4_pairwise_comparison_contrast_matr.description: Construct a contrast matrix for the three pairwise comparisons defined in Module3. The contrast matrix is built using makeContrasts() with the following comparisons:

comparisons.name: FE_vs_CD
comparisons.biological_question: How does high iron diet reshape the metabolic response during CDI?

comparisons.name: CD_vs_NC
comparisons.biological_question: What is the baseline metabolic signature of C. difficile infection?
expected_metabolites: Primary bile acids ↑, secondary bile acids ↓ (microbiota disruption)

comparisons.name: FE_vs_NC
comparisons.biological_question: Does high iron intervention restore metabolic homeostasis?
expected_metabolites: Partial recovery of CDI-perturbed metabolites toward NC levels
Unique protective metabolic features (TUDCA elevation, proline depletion)

step_4_pairwise_comparison_contrast_matr.notes: For FE_vs_CD, positive logFC means higher in FE (high iron + CDI) than in CD (CDI alone). For CD_vs_NC, positive logFC means higher in CD. For FE_vs_NC, positive logFC means higher in FE.

step_5_limma_moderated_t_test.description: Apply empirical Bayes moderation (eBayes) to shrink variances toward a common value, improving power for small-sample experiments. For each pairwise comparison, extract results using topTable(). The moderated t-test identifies metabolites with significant fold-changes between two groups while borrowing strength across all2059 metabolites for variance estimation.
step_5_limma_moderated_t_test.method: contrasts.fit(fit, contrast_matrix) → eBayes() → topTable(..., coef=i, sort.by='P', number=Inf)
step_5_limma_moderated_t_test.output_per_comparison: diff_<comparison>_all.csv with columns: molecule, logFC, AveExpr, t, P.Value, adj.P.Val, B

step_6_significance_filtering_and_rankin.description: Apply the specified filtering thresholds to extract significant metabolites for each comparison. Since no logFC cutoff is specified, significance is based solely on adjusted p-value. The top500 molecules are selected by descending |logFC| for downstream visualization and ranking.
step_6_significance_filtering_and_rankin.output_per_comparison: diff_<comparison>_significant.csv with additional columns: regulation (up/down), rank_by_logFC
step_6_significance_filtering_and_rankin.notes: The top500 by |logFC| is applied AFTER filtering by adj.P.Val <0.05. If fewer than500 metabolites pass the p-value filter, all significant metabolites are included.

step_7_vip_integration_for_metabolomics.description: For metabolomics data specifically, integrate PLS-DA Variable Importance in Projection (VIP) scores from Module2 as an additional confidence metric. VIP >1 indicates metabolites with above-average contribution to group discrimination in the PLS-DA model. The combined LIMMA + VIP results are used for a consensus ranking where metabolites meeting BOTH adj.P.Val <0.05 (from limma) AND VIP >1 (from PLS-DA) are designated as 'high-confidence differential metabolites'.
step_7_vip_integration_for_metabolomics.vip_source: Module2 PLS-DA analysis (828 metabolites with VIP >1 identified)
step_7_vip_integration_for_metabolomics.integration_method: Merge LIMMA results with pre-computed VIP scores by molecule ID

consensus_categories.expected_count: ~800-828 metabolites (based on Module2 finding of818 key candidates)

consensus_categories.interpretation: Metabolites significant by limma but with modest contribution to PLS-DA separation
Metabolites important for group separation but not meeting parametric significance threshold

step_7_vip_integration_for_metabolomics.output: vip_integrated_results.csv with columns: molecule, logFC, adj.P.Val, VIP_score, consensus_category

step_8_top500_logFC_ranking_across_compa.description: For each comparison, extract the top500 metabolites by |logFC| (descending) among those with adj.P.Val <0.05. Create a combined ranking table and expression profile matrix. This facilitates identification of the most impactful metabolites driving each comparison and allows cross-comparison analysis.

parameters.ranking_method: |logFC| descending, then adj.P.Val ascending for ties

direction_mapping.FE_vs_CD: positive = higher in FE (high iron + CDI)
direction_mapping.FE_vs_NC: positive = higher in FE (high iron + CDI)

step_8_top500_logFC_ranking_across_compa.output: top500_logFC_ranked.csv with combined data for all three comparisons

step_9_volcano_plot_visualization.description: Generate volcano plots for each pairwise comparison showing -log10(adj.P.Val) vs log2(Fold Change). Key features: (1) Color by significance (adj.P.Val <0.05 = red, non-significant = grey); (2) Label top10-15 metabolites by combined rank of |logFC| and significance; (3) Include horizontal dashed line at -log10(0.05); (4) Include vertical dashed lines at logFC = ±0 (if no FC cutoff) or at meaningful biological thresholds.

step_10_venn_and_upset_analysis.description: Generate Venn diagram and UpSet plot to visualize the overlap and uniqueness of significant metabolites across the three comparisons. This is critical for identifying: (1) metabolites uniquely altered by high iron diet (FE vs CD only); (2) metabolites changed by CDI regardless of diet (CD vs NC ∩ FE vs CD); (3) metabolites that show 'rescue' patterns (significant in CD vs NC but not in FE vs NC).
expected_biological_insights: FE_vs_CD ∩ CD_vs_NC ∩ FE_vs_NC: Metabolites varying across all conditions (housekeeping metabolic perturbations)
FE_vs_CD ONLY: Metabolites uniquely altered by high iron diet in CDI context (e.g., TUDCA, proline) — most biologically interesting
CD_vs_NC ONLY: CDI-specific metabolic signature independent of iron diet
FE_vs_NC ∩ CD_vs_NC: CDI-perturbed metabolites that are NOT rescued by high iron diet (persistent dysregulation)

step_11_heatmap_visualization.description: Generate clustered heatmaps of differential metabolite expression across all18 samples. Multiple heatmap versions for different subsets: (1) All significant metabolites across three comparisons; (2) Top50 metabolites by |logFC|; (3) FE vs CD significant metabolites only. Row annotations from metabolites.csv (super_class, class, sub_class) will be added if available. Column annotations for group assignment.

heatmap_types.name: all_groups_all_significant
heatmap_types.data: expression matrix rows = union of significant metabolites from all3 comparisons
heatmap_types.clustering: rows = hierarchical (ward.D2), columns = by group order

heatmap_types.name: top50_logFC
heatmap_types.data: top50 metabolites across all comparisons by |logFC|

heatmap_types.name: FE_vs_CD_only
heatmap_types.data: significant metabolites from FE vs CD comparison only
heatmap_types.clustering: rows = hierarchical, columns = by group order

heatmap_types.name: top500_expression_profiles
heatmap_types.data: top500 metabolites by |logFC| from each comparison combined
heatmap_types.clustering: rows = hierarchical (ward.D2), columns = by group order

step_12_cross_comparison_correlation_ana.description: Perform cross-comparison analysis to identify metabolic trend patterns across the three groups. For each metabolite, compute the mean expression in each group (NC, CD, FE) and classify into trend categories. This is essential for understanding the 'rescue' vs 'unique protection' vs 'persistent dysregulation' patterns described in Module3.

methods: Compute group means for each metabolite
Define trend patterns based on direction of change

pattern_categories.definition: CD differs from NC (|logFC| >0, adj.P <0.05 in CD_vs_NC) AND FE differs from CD toward NC direction (FE_vs_CD opposite sign to CD_vs_NC)
pattern_categories.biological_meaning: High iron diet partially or fully reverses CDI-induced metabolic changes

pattern_categories.definition: FE significantly differs from CD (FE_vs_CD significant) but CD does NOT differ from NC (CD_vs_NC not significant)
pattern_categories.biological_meaning: High iron diet induces protective metabolites that are not part of the CDI baseline response

pattern_categories.definition: Both CD_vs_NC and FE_vs_NC are significant with same direction (FE does not rescue)
pattern_categories.biological_meaning: Metabolic perturbations that persist despite high iron intervention

pattern_categories.definition: FE significantly differs from BOTH CD and NC (both FE_vs_CD and FE_vs_NC significant)
pattern_categories.biological_meaning: Metabolites uniquely altered by high iron diet regardless of infection context

step_12_cross_comparison_correlation_ana.output: Cross-comparison trend classification added to combined_diff_results.csv

step_13_pvalue_distribution_diagnostics.description: Generate p-value distribution histograms for each comparison to assess model assumptions and detect potential biases. A well-calibrated test should produce a uniform distribution of p-values for non-significant metabolites (under the null), with a peak near zero for truly differential metabolites.
diagnostic_plots: Histogram of raw p-values (all2059 metabolites) for each comparison
Q-Q plot of p-values against uniform distribution
Histogram of B-statistics (log-odds of differential expression)

step_14_save_comprehensive_results.description: Compile all results into comprehensive output tables and generate the module conclusion report. The combined results table will merge LIMMA statistics from all three comparisons with metabolite annotations (metabolites.csv) and VIP scores, providing a one-stop resource for downstream pathway enrichment and biological interpretation.

time_series_handling.reason: This is a cross-sectional study with a single time point per group. All samples were collected at the same endpoint post-infection. No time covariate is included in the model. If time-series data becomes available in the future, the design matrix can be extended to include a time factor (e.g., model.matrix(~0 + group + time)) and the primary contrast of interest would be the group:time interaction term to identify metabolites with differential temporal trajectories.

statistical_parameters.logFC_cutoff: None (include all significant metabolites regardless of fold change magnitude)

vip_integration_parameters.vip_integration_purpose: Metabolomics-specific: VIP scores provide a multivariate complement to univariate limma results. Metabolites with both adj.P.Val <0.05 and VIP >1 are designated as 'high-confidence' candidates for pathway enrichment.
vip_integration_parameters.expected_vip_sig_overlap: Based on Module2:818 metabolites (39.7%) had both VIP >1 and FDR <0.05 by ANOVA

key_metabolite_pathways_to_monitor.expected_in_FE_vs_CD: TUDCA up in FE; secondary bile acids altered

checks.action: Verify that the design matrix is full rank (no linear dependencies among columns)
Check residual distribution for normality assumptions of limma (should be approximately normal due to log2 transformation)
Check if NC1 (which showed deviation in PCA) exerts undue leverage on results. Compare results with and without NC1.
Confirm p-value histogram is approximately uniform for non-significant metabolites
Verify that the observed number of significant metabolites is plausible given the expected proportion of truly differential metabolites

notes: SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS: (1) This is a METABOLOMICS dataset — not transcriptomics. The limma framework is equally applicable but the interpretation differs: we identify differential metabolites rather than differentially expressed genes. VIP score integration from PLS-DA is a standard metabolomics practice. (2) The preprocessed expression matrix is already log2-transformed and median-centered (confirmed in Module1), so AveExpr values in limma output will be centered around zero — this is expected and correct. (3) No logFC cutoff is applied as per user specification, but the top500 by |logFC| ranking will naturally prioritize metabolites with large fold changes. (4) The three comparisons are NOT independent — FE vs CD and FE vs NC share the FE group. This overlap is addressed in the Venn/UpSet analysis and cross-comparison trend classification. (5) The primary comparison (FE vs CD) should be emphasized in interpretation as it most directly addresses the research question about high iron diet's effect on CDI. (6) The metabolites.csv annotation file with KEGG, HMDB, LIPID MAPS, super_class, class, sub_class (32 fields,2172 records) will enable comprehensive biological annotation of differential metabolites. (7) Time-series analysis is NOT applicable — this is a single time-point cross-sectional design. If future studies include multiple time points, a separate limma analysis with time:group interaction terms would be needed.
